purpose: Provides a shock-type-identified allocation framework that adapts portfolio construction to the current correlation regime, answering: how should multi-asset portfolios be positioned given identified shock type?
components: Demand shock identification → long duration, standard 60/40 allocation (bonds hedge equities)
Supply shock identification → short duration + commodities, reduced bond allocation (bonds amplify equity losses)
Mixed/uncertain shock environment → barbell construction (short-duration safe assets + commodities + selective equity exposure)
Drawdown magnitude non-linearity: bonds may still rally in severe drawdowns (>15%) but fail in moderate stress (5-10%), changing optimal hedge construction
current_assessment: Current environment is mixed/uncertain — oscillating between growth scares and inflation scares with output gap near zero. Framework recommends barbell positioning with lower bond duration than traditional 60/40. The 60/40 performance of 2023-2025 (+12%, +14%, +8%) demonstrates that positive correlation does not guarantee poor returns, but catastrophic failure occurs under positive correlation AND negative returns (stagflation).
limitations: Shock identification in real-time is difficult — lags of weeks to months before dominant shock type is clear
Does not account for regime transition dynamics (overshooting, mean reversion, positioning feedback loops)
The 'both paths lead to positive correlation' insight (crisis path and repression path) limits hedge options
Vol-targeting and risk-parity mechanical effects can overwhelm the framework's signal
